item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here, and is licensed under an NC "non-commercial" license. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. So Caverage Co was what sold you on it? What exactly is Caverage Co? I don't get the reference.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Sorry it took so long for me to respond. I'm going to object to linking this to my Broken Light series, just because I haven't written the part that involves this SCP yet. 2483 I'm okay with, since that one does feature into the series already, but until I get over my writer's block I'm not ready for 1562, 2041, or 2224 to be linked to it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits below were not made per author request.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

SCP-2224 is one of several SCPs used as the focal point of the Broken Light tale series. All the articles used in this series are located at Site-24. Since this particular site lacks a facility dossier, an inline link to the Site-24 mention to the broken light hub would be a suitable crosslink. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-2224 is located in the External Environmental Lab on Site 24. Level 2 security clearance or higher is required to access the object, in addition to permission from Dr. Darritz.

To this:

SCP-2224 is located in the External Environmental Lab on Site 24. Level 2 security clearance or higher is required to access the object, in addition to permission from Dr. Darritz.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correct, but the item itself was created in Denmark. I had started a tale series that would have detailed how this object and the other playground equipment created by the same Danish person ended up in other nearby countries. Unfortunately I got writer's block something fierce when writing the 4th part, and haven't revisited it in a long time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doesn't quite grab me.

Neutral vote.

Also, the Finnish language dies not use Øø, which is exclusive to Norwegian, Danish, Faroese and Southern Sami languages. Finnish, Swedish and Icelandic (as well as several other languages) use Öö instead, which is pronounced the exact same way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote, the idea is alright but the anomalous effect has a poor tone.
Furthermore, this is a legit piece on the main site and I was doubting to give an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice and simple. felt like an old SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the interviews quite boring, but the tests launching D's were hillarious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this kind of stuff.

(Collapsed for referencing something spoiler'd.)
– hide block

The way the "Ø" seat malfunctions reminds me a lot of the way video games malfunction (like, I can totally imagine the texture on an object being replaced with the image of the Nintendo logo) so it felt strangely authentic, like it has a reasonable internal logic.


So, good job! I hope we see more from this maker of fine playground equipment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch, thanks! I will fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please see Statement 2224-2 for the transcript of D-119786's written account.

Yet the collapsible(sp?) says Statement 2224-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you're right about the tone for that line. I may change it.

I'm not sure how I feel about the transcript of the audio log. I questioned it myself originally, but you're the first person to have mentioned having a problem with it so far. I'm not against changing it, as I don't entirely disagree with what you're saying, but I like having that characterization sometimes. If others voice disapproval, I may edit out the accent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the written statement by D-119786 you say "All spelling and grammar mistakes have been left in."

Firstly, I think the tone would be better preserved by saying something like "Spelling and grammar of original unchanged".

Secondly, in the interview, you introduce spelling mistakes to indicate D-120048's accent and social class. That rarely happens in real transcripts, for me it breaks suspension of disbelief.

It's a good skip overall and I like the idea of a series of playground anomalies. Childhood is the source of much of the best creep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was not going to upvote until the end of that interview. Nice stinger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty well written. First time I saw it on the chat, I thought it was going to be an OTT item, but there's enough of a twist here to make it interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I didn't even consider the similarities between this and your SCP-1932, but I can kind of see it now that you mention it. And while I definitely remembered your SCP because it was posted minutes before I posted SCP-1562, I can promise that any similarities are unintentional. I know you're not making an accusation, just wanted to acknowledge what you are saying.

+ spoilers
– hide block

Look at the lethality this way: The "A" seat functions exactly as intended. It bends reality for just the user, to provide a little extra fun, though it's certainly questionable if children would enjoy that. The "Ø" seat is defective/broken; so it will function properly if the rider on the "D" seat pushes off at the proper moment. Its effects only become extreme after the "D" rider stays in the ground position for too long. Any deaths were brought about due to misuse of a broken object, and not because of any malicious intent by its creator, or hokey attempts at making it grimdark by me. The other world isn't some wacky dimension, it's a broken reality from a defective plaything, that only affects the rider of that seat. Like my other 2 playground SCPs, it's a well-intentioned failure created by…a well-intentioned failure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good, though maybe a little similar to my SCP-1932 in that it is playground equipment that gives you hallucinations of height and weird scary stuff but maybe they're not hallucinations and they're real for the person experiencing them, but that doesn't actually bother me at all - I can tell it's not copied.

I do think maybe making it be quite so lethal is a step too far, and I think personally I would have preferred it without the weird other reality that people experience (that is, if it just made you think you'd gone up in the air), but that's not to say that it's not interesting and well-written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed this one. Good job! I look forward to seeing what you're planning to build off of from here! \(^_^)/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I'm jut saying that if you add more interesting context to that company, I would live to see it become a GOI, although I understand if you don't want it to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I don't have any intentions of trying to force a new GOI, just establishing an over-arcing story in my head that links my playground-themed SCPs. It's more to give some context to a series of tales I'd like to write in the near future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huge thanks to SoullessSingularity and Zolgamax for giving me the thorough critique I desperately needed on this. Many thanks to anyone else who read the draft and gave advice in chat.

I struggled with the language majorly on this one, but I hope it doesn't suffer too much because of it. Tone and/or wording is probably still awkward in places, but I've done the best I can do for now.

EDIT: Image was taken from www.freepik.com. It took forever to find an image that didn't require paying for a license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting idea, well written, and I almost wanna see that group become a GOI because they sound very interesting.
